_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn about different kinds of criteria — like desired qualities or rules — and how criteria help us make judgements when reasoning.

_source.w: We learn about different kinds of criteria — like desired qualities or rules — and how criteria help us make judgements when reasoning.
y: Criteria give us a fair and consistent basis for making judgements.
Understanding where criteria come from helps us choose or create appropriate ones.
Using criteria moves our thinking from 'I feel' to 'I can demonstrate'.
eg: We identify the criteria our teacher gave us and use them to evaluate our work.
We discuss the difference between criteria based on rules (safety requirements) and criteria based on qualities (creativity).
We use criteria to compare two options and decide which one is better.
We create our own criteria for judging something — like what makes a good story.
We check whether our criteria are fair and apply equally to all options.
We use criteria to give specific, useful feedback on a classmate's work.
